summary: My name is Rodrigo Botero Ibarra, born in Cali, Colombia in 1970. I studied topographic engineering at Universidad del Valle and worked as a topographer and university instructor. Later, I pursued a master's degree at Universidad Javeriana and am currently applying for a PhD in mathematics at the University of Minnesota. I moved to Minnesota in 2014 with my family to continue my studies and be close to my parents and siblings. My goal is to advance my academic career and eventually return to Colombia to work in research.

Cali is my hometown in Colombia, a small and warm city where I have lived almost my entire life. The green environment here reminds me a lot of Cali, which I appreciate especially during the summer. It shaped much of my early life and education before I moved abroad. I feel a strong connection to Cali, and despite my studies in the United States, I plan to return there to contribute through research. It’s a place full of memories and warmth for me and my family.

Colombia is where I was born and where I spent most of my life, including my education and early career. I studied at Universidad Javeriana and Universidad del Valle, and worked as a topographer and university instructor there. However, I found that pursuing a PhD in mathematics at my age is very difficult in Colombia due to cultural and institutional barriers. Despite these challenges, I hope to return to Colombia in the future to work in research and contribute to the academic community. Colombia remains my home and the place I want to give back to after my studies abroad.

I graduated from Universidad del Valle with a degree in topographic engineering. It was my first public university experience after attending Universidad Javeriana. At Universidad del Valle, I gained the technical skills that allowed me to work as a topographer and later as a university instructor. This institution played a key role in shaping my professional foundation in Colombia. It represents an important chapter in my academic and professional journey.

I first attended the private Universidad Javeriana before moving to Universidad del Valle. Currently, I am finishing my master's degree there, working on a thesis in computer systems. Universidad Javeriana has been important for my advanced studies beyond my undergraduate degree. It has helped me deepen my knowledge and prepare for my PhD application abroad. This university remains a key part of my academic development in Colombia.

The University of Minnesota is where I came in 2014 to study English and pursue a PhD in mathematics. It was the first university I got to know in the United States, and I was impressed by its academic environment and the kindness of the people. I am currently studying English there to meet the high language requirements for the PhD program, which also involves teaching responsibilities. The university represents an opportunity for me to advance my research career in a place with cutting-edge knowledge. I am waiting for my PhD application decision and hope to contribute to this institution soon.

Minnesota is where my family and I currently live, alongside my parents who also reside here. I was encouraged to come here because almost all my family is in Minnesota, and I appreciate the peaceful and green environment that reminds me of Cali. The community here is very kind and different from what I experienced in Colombia. Minnesota has become my new home as I study and prepare for my PhD. It offers a supportive atmosphere for my academic and family life.
